response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable senior software engineer with strong full-stack and low-latency systems experience, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, and deep-learning workload optimization at the silicon level — none of which appear anywhere in his profile. His background is entirely in web, mobile, and distributed software engineering. While he has ~16 years of software experience satisfying the education/experience threshold, the skill set mismatch with the core technical requirements is fundamental and not bridgeable by seniority alone.
strengths: 16+ years of software engineering experience with demonstrated ability to work across the stack and at multiple seniority levels
Exposure to performance-sensitive systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O, high-performance databases) shows some performance engineering instinct
Embedded and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms at Altom, Sound Position Server with multi-speaker audio synthesis) indicates ability to work outside pure web software
concerns: Zero demonstrated experience with SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware-software co-design — the core technical requirement of this role
No background in AI/ML hardware optimization, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD pipelines, or deep-learning workload analysis at any level
Role requires Principal-level hardware architecture leadership; candidate's entire career trajectory is software engineering with no signal of transition toward computer architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
